decks.notes: The main concept of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Ocelot Pride, along with efficient removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Thraben Charm. The deck also includes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and synergistic cards like Goblin Bombardment and Voice of Victory to maintain pressure and close out games quickly. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive game plan while also providing flexibility with cards like Arid Mesa and Marsh Flats.

This deck focuses on using instant spells to reanimate powerful creatures like Griselbrand, while also disrupting the opponent with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation. Ephemerate and Faithful Mending provide card advantage and the ability to protect key creatures. Quantum Riddler and Psychic Frog provide additional value while setting up for the combo. The deck also includes versatile removal spells like Prismatic Ending and Solitude to deal with opposing threats. The goal is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful reanimated creatures.

The main concept of this Aggro-Affinity deck is to quickly assemble a board presence using artifacts like Urza's Saga, Mox Opal, and Engineered Explosives. By using cards like Pinnacle Emissary and Kappa Cannoneer, the deck aims to apply pressure early on while disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Tormod's Crypt and Metallic Rebuke. Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Arcbound Ravager provide card advantage and synergy with the artifact-heavy strategy. The deck also includes disruption tools like Claws of Gix and Pithing Needle to control the opponent's options and secure victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy by countering key spells, removing threats, and drawing cards to stay ahead. Wan Shi Tong, Librarian provides card advantage, while Solitude offers efficient removal. Teferi, Time Raveler helps to control the tempo of the game, while Supreme Verdict serves as a board wipe. The deck also includes a variety of lands to fix mana and disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around denying the opponent's resources and maintaining control of the game.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays with a variety of creatures and spells to pressure the opponent quickly. It utilizes a mix of efficient creatures like Ragavan, Territorial Kavu, and Kroxa, along with efficient removal like Lightning Bolt and Leyline Binding to control the board and clear the way for attacks. The deck also includes some disruptive elements like Stubborn Denial and Consign to Memory to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is carefully crafted to support the 4-5 color aggro strategy, allowing for flexibility in casting spells of different colors.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifact creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards like Mox Opal, Urza's Saga, and Mishra's Bauble to generate mana and card advantage. The creatures such as Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary provide a fast clock and pressure on the opponent. Engineered Explosives and Metallic Rebuke help control the board while Emry, Lurker of the Loch can provide recursion. The inclusion of Claws of Gix and Welding Jar offer protection and flexibility. Overall, the deck aims to deploy threats quickly and efficiently to win the game.

This deck focuses on aggressive plays and synergy with artifact creatures. The key strategy is to quickly deploy artifacts such as Mox Opal and Urza's Saga to power out threats like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary. Efficient removal such as Engineered Explosives and Tormod's Crypt disrupt opponents while cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch provide card advantage and recursion. Welding Jar protects key pieces and Metallic Rebuke helps to keep opponents on their toes. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a combination of speed, power, and disruption.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing a variety of spells and counterspells to manage the opponent's threats. The key strategy involves using cards like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Teferi, Time Raveler to control the board, while also utilizing Isochron Scepter and Lórien Revealed for additional spellcasting capabilities. The deck also includes removal spells like Supreme Verdict and Prismatic Ending to deal with opposing threats. Overall, the deck aims to outlast the opponent and secure victory through careful planning and strategic use of resources.

The deck focuses on quickly generating mana using spells like Pyretic Ritual and Manamorphose to cast powerful spells and combo off. Ral, Monsoon Mage's ability to copy spells and generate card advantage pairs well with Past in Flames, allowing for explosive turns. Glimpse the Impossible can also help dig for combo pieces. Additionally, Wrenn's Resolve helps protect the combo pieces from removal spells. The deck aims to quickly assemble the combo pieces and win the game in a single turn by generating a large storm count with spells like Grapeshot.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on aggression and tempo, utilizing cards like Ajani, Ragavan, and Guide of Souls to quickly establish a dominant board presence. The deck also includes removal options like Lightning Bolt and Goblin Bombardment to control the opponent's board. With a mix of creatures, spells, and land cards, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and relentless offense while also maintaining some versatility and resilience through cards like Ranger-Captain of Eos and Voice of Victory.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly disrupt your opponent's hand with cards like Inquisition of Kozilek and Thoughtseize, while also controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push and Kozilek's Command. The deck also aims to apply pressure with aggressive creatures like Nethergoyf and Emperor of Bones. Additionally, the inclusion of Eldrazi Temple and Thought-Knot Seer allows for additional disruption and tempo plays. The deck's land base is designed to support its color requirements and maximize its potential with cards like Urborg, Tomb of Yawgmoth and Urza's Saga.

This deck focuses on efficiently ramping up mana with Birthing Ritual and utilizing powerful Simic creatures like Shardless Agent, Harbinger of the Seas, and Ice-Fang Coatl. It also disrupts opponents' strategies with cards like Flare of Denial and Force of Negation. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells, while also providing card advantage through the use of Coiling Oracle and Endurance. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements and maximize ramp potential.

The main concept of this deck is to use the combo between Basking Broodscale and Blade of the Bloodchief to generate a massive amount of +1/+1 counters on your creatures. By utilizing cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Eldrazi Temple to ramp up your mana, you can quickly overwhelm your opponent with powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End. Additionally, cards like Urza's Saga and Boseiju, Who Endures provide additional utility and protection for your combo pieces. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures and powerful synergies.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly attacking the opponent with low-cost creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Monastery Swiftspear, while also using burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to control the board and finish off opponents. The deck utilizes cantrips like Preordain and Mishra's Bauble to keep the card advantage flowing and maintain tempo. Its versatility is enhanced by cards like Expressive Iteration and Academic Dispute, while cards like Cori-Steel Cutter and Boomerang Basics provide disruption and protection. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents with aggressive creatures and fast-paced plays.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to have a fast and aggressive playstyle, utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler to quickly pressure the opponent. The deck also includes spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart for direct damage and removal, as well as card draw and manipulation tools like Preordain and Mishra's Bauble to maintain consistency. By combining burn spells with efficient creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with early aggression and efficient card advantage to secure a swift victory.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly and aggressively overwhelm the opponent with cheap and efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, supported by burn spells such as Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent through constant threats and efficient card draw with cards like Expressive Iteration and Mishra's Bauble. By combining aggression with card advantage, the deck seeks to control the pace of the game and secure a quick victory.
